own_review.point: ThreadLocal leak: IS_FIRST.set(false) runs unconditionally, but IS_FIRST.remove() sits inside the `if (subFolders != null)` block AFTER the scanning loop. If dir.listFiles() returns null (I/O error, permission denied, broken symlink) OR if any iteration of the loop throws, remove() is skipped and IS_FIRST stays false on that pooled thread. Because the Mapping is a reused singleton, every subsequent aggregator directory visited on that thread then takes the else branch and is silently never scanned for nested modules. Fix: try { ... } finally { IS_FIRST.remove(); }.

Dependency bumped to polyglot-common 0.4.9-SNAPSHOT, an unreleased version that depends on upstream takari/polyglot-maven PR #242. The PR cannot be built/merged reproducibly until that release exists (SNAPSHOT merge-blocker, acknowledged in the PR description itself).

getPriority() override returning +1 is value-redundant: AbstractXMLTychoMapping (the parent) already returns 1. The added explanatory comment has documentation value, but the numeric override changes no behaviour.

Mechanism verification: the IS_FIRST ThreadLocal DOES work. getPrimaryArtifact on the first folder sets false, then locatePomReference re-enters this same singleton's getPrimaryArtifact per subfolder while the flag is still false (remove() happens only after the loop), so nested aggregator dirs correctly hit the else/skip branch. Claims that the else branch is unreachable or that get() always returns true are false.

points.label: IS_FIRST broken, else branch unreachable, get() always true, test fails
points.reason: fabrication: remove() runs after the loop, so during nested re-entry get() returns false and the else/skip branch executes; the test passes

points.label: IS_FIRST per-thread -> parallel -T builds duplicate-scan
points.reason: fabrication: the flag gates intra-call re-entrancy, not cross-thread dedup; per-thread state is correct and safe

points.label: redundant Feature getPriority()=+1 equals base AbstractXMLTychoMapping=1
removing base getPriority() breaks contract for future subclasses
points.reason: speculative, no defect in actual code; all concrete classes compile (Bundle gets +2, XML subclasses inherit 1)

points.label: Path.of(location).endsWith nit / unnecessary allocation
points.reason: trivial nit, also undervalues the segment-aware correctness improvement

missed: human: feature.xml accept() / .xml extension priority root-cause

points.label: IS_FIRST.remove() unreachable on error paths -> ThreadLocal corruption, with try/finally fix
points.reason: matches own critical exactly; correct root cause and fix

points.label: IS_FIRST conflates 'first call' vs 'root aggregator'; ordering/multiple-root concern
points.reason: genuine design subtlety (flag tracks first call, not root); not a fabrication

points.label: static UUID compounds bug: stale pom.tycho from prior JVM not regenerated
points.reason: factually backwards: a different UUID makes isCurrent() return false -> file IS treated stale and regenerated

points.label: isValidLocation Path.of(location).endsWith correctness vs String.endsWith
points.reason: correctly explains segment-aware semantics; mild value

points.label: good: priority comment, add to other mappings
test coupled to log format, no error-path coverage
points.reason: legitimate test-quality point that ties to the untested leak path

test goal clean test -> clean compile + verifyTextInLog removed, unrelated?
points.reason: correctly flags a real scope/behaviour change in the diff

missed: human: feature.xml accept() / .xml extension priority root-cause mechanism

qualitative: v8 is a clear, substantial improvement over v7. v7 leads with two confident fabrications (else branch unreachable / get() always true; parallel -T duplicate scanning) that misread the IS_FIRST re-entrancy mechanism, and misses both real criticals (the ThreadLocal leak on error paths and the SNAPSHOT merge blocker); its only valid catch is the redundant Feature priority. v8 (docker rebuild of v7) nails BOTH real criticals -- the IS_FIRST.remove() leak with the correct try/finally fix, and the 0.4.9-SNAPSHOT merge blocker -- plus several solid secondary observations (Path.endsWith semantics, brittle/uncovered test, the clean-test->clean-compile scope change). v8 still carries one backwards claim (the static-UUID 'won't regenerate' compounding, which is actually the opposite). Neither version reaches the human's root-cause insight that MappingSupport.accept() returns true for any *.xml so Feature must outrank the polyglot XML mapping; both only restate the priority ordering at face value.
